meaning: n.世俗论者,非宗教的教育论者
examples: Only the Atheist can be a consistent Secularist.
Mr Allawi is a moderate but, unlike Mr Chalabi, he is no secularist.
Egypt’s Mubarak, Tunisia’s Ben Ali, and Syria’s Assad built brutal (and deeply illiberal) police states with strong secularist support.
Musharraf's declaration of emergency rule also has strained relations with Bhutto, who shares his secularist, pro-Western views.
The secularist establishment, which includes the army, courts and universities, is opposed to any reform of the ban.
